mission.title: Install and Configure Xregistry on Kubernetes
mission.description: xRegistry is a metadata registry for discovering, validating, and versioning resources in distributed systems. Installing xRegistry allows you to manage your resources effectively through its server and CLI implementations.

steps.title: Clone the xRegistry repository
steps.description:
First, clone the xRegistry repository to your local machine using the following command:
```bash
git clone https://github.com/xregistry/server.git
cd server
```

steps.title: Deploy xRegistry using kubectl
steps.description:
Apply the Kubernetes manifests to deploy xRegistry. Ensure you are in the `server` directory:
```bash
kubectl apply -f k8s/deployment.yaml --create-namespace --namespace xregistry
```

steps.title: Verify the deployment
steps.description:
Check if the xRegistry pods are running successfully:
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace xregistry
```

steps.title: Expose the xRegistry service
steps.description:
To access the xRegistry service, you can use port forwarding:
```bash
kubectl port-forward svc/xregistry-service 8080:80 --namespace xregistry
```

steps.title: Post-install configuration
steps.description:
Configure resource limits for the xRegistry deployment by editing the deployment manifest:
```bash
kubectl edit deployment xregistry --namespace xregistry
``` 
Add resource limits under the `spec.containers.resources` section.

uninstall.title: Remove the xRegistry service
uninstall.description:
First, delete the xRegistry deployment and service:
```bash
kubectl delete -f k8s/deployment.yaml --namespace xregistry
```

uninstall.title: Clean up the xRegistry namespace
uninstall.description:
Remove the xRegistry namespace to clean up all associated resources:
```bash
kubectl delete namespace xregistry
```

upgrade.title: Backup current state
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, back up the current configuration and data if necessary. You can export the current deployment:
```bash
kubectl get deployment xregistry -n xregistry -o yaml > xregistry-backup.yaml
```

upgrade.title: Update the repository
upgrade.description:
Pull the latest changes from the xRegistry repository:
```bash
git pull origin main
```

upgrade.title: Upgrade the deployment
upgrade.description:
Reapply the updated Kubernetes manifests:
```bash
kubectl apply -f k8s/deployment.yaml --namespace xregistry
```

upgrade.title: Verify the upgrade
upgrade.description:
Check the status of the pods to ensure they are running the new version:
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace xregistry
```

troubleshooting.title: Pods not starting
troubleshooting.description:
If the pods are not starting, check the logs for errors:
```bash
kubectl logs <pod-name> --namespace xregistry
``` 
Ensure that all required environment variables and configurations are set correctly.

troubleshooting.title: Service not reachable
troubleshooting.description:
If you cannot access the service, verify that the service is running:
```bash
kubectl get svc --namespace xregistry
``` 
Ensure that the correct ports are exposed and that port forwarding is set up correctly.

troubleshooting.title: Insufficient resources
troubleshooting.description:
If you encounter resource allocation issues, check the resource limits set in the deployment:
```bash
kubectl describe deployment xregistry --namespace xregistry
``` 
Adjust the resource limits in the deployment manifest if necessary.

resolution.summary: A successful installation of xRegistry will have all pods running without errors, and you will be able to access the service through the specified port. You can manage your resources using the CLI provided.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running and kubectl configured to interact with it.
